evals.prompt: How should I share my new SaaS product on Indie Hackers to get feedback and users?
evals.expected_output: Recommends creating a product page, spending time engaging with the community before posting, writing a Show IH post with story structure (problem, solution, metrics, specific ask), posting mid-week, and responding to all comments.

assertions.name: post_structure
assertions.description: Recommends a structured approach to the product share post, such as including the problem being solved, metrics or progress, and a specific ask for the community

assertions.name: pre_engagement
assertions.description: Advises engaging with the IH community (commenting, helping others) before or alongside promoting their own product

assertions.name: engagement_tactics
assertions.description: Mentions at least one concrete engagement tactic like responding to comments, posting mid-week, or using the Show IH format

evals.prompt: Is Indie Hackers worth it for SEO backlinks? How does the link value compare to Product Hunt?
evals.expected_output: Explains IH has DR75 but backlinks are nofollow, so no SEO link equity is passed. Compares with Product Hunt (DR91 dofollow). Recommends IH for community engagement and high conversion rates rather than SEO backlinks.

assertions.name: nofollow_explained
assertions.description: States that Indie Hackers backlinks are nofollow and explains this means no direct SEO link equity

assertions.name: ph_comparison
assertions.description: Compares IH's backlink value with Product Hunt, noting PH provides dofollow links with higher DR

assertions.name: community_value
assertions.description: Explains that IH's primary value is community engagement, founder networking, or high conversion rates rather than SEO backlinks

evals.prompt: Should I launch on Indie Hackers or Product Hunt first?
evals.expected_output: Compares the two platforms: IH is ongoing engagement with high conversion (~24%), PH is a 24-hour competitive sprint (~3% conversion). Recommends IH first to build community and refine messaging, then PH as a polished launch event.

assertions.name: platform_comparison
assertions.description: Compares key differences between Indie Hackers and Product Hunt such as engagement style, competition level, conversion rates, or audience

assertions.name: sequencing_recommendation
assertions.description: Recommends an order for using the platforms, with reasoning for which to use first

assertions.name: conversion_insight
assertions.description: Mentions that IH tends to have higher conversion or engagement rates for indie/bootstrapped products compared to PH's broader but less targeted audience
